ui.bank_tip: Pick a question. Focus on boundaries, conditions, and shrinking the search space.
ui.editor_tip: Paste starter code into the editor. Test edge cases carefully.

questions.title: Binary Search Basics
questions.prompt:
Given a sorted array and a target value, return the index of the target if found. Otherwise return -1.

You must solve it in O(log n) time.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Binary search basics"><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Binary Search Basics</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Compare target with middle element and discard half each step</text><g font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="56" y="126" width="96" height="64" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="104" y="168" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="168" y="126" width="96" height="64" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="216" y="168" text-anchor="middle">3</text><rect x="280" y="116" width="96" height="84" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.34)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="328" y="168" text-anchor="middle">5</text><rect x="392" y="126" width="96" height="64" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="440" y="168" text-anchor="middle">7</text><rect x="504" y="126" width="96" height="64" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="552" y="168" text-anchor="middle">9</text></g><text x="304" y="224" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">mid = 5</text><path d="M 640 154 L 738 154" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 726 142 L 738 154 L 726 166" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="756" y="116" width="170" height="78" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.18)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="841" y="148" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">target = 7</text><text x="841" y="176" text-anchor="middle" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">7 > 5 → go right</text><text x="30" y="282" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Key idea: low, high, mid</text></svg>
hints: Keep two pointers: low and high.
Find middle using mid = (low + high) // 2.
If target == arr[mid], return mid.
If target is larger, search right half; otherwise search left half.
questions.starter_code:
def binary_search(arr, target):
    # TODO
    return -1

print(binary_search([1, 3, 5, 7, 9], 7))
print(binary_search([1, 3, 5, 7, 9], 4))

questions.title: First and Last Occurrence
questions.prompt:
Given a sorted array and a target, return the first and last occurrence of the target.

If the target is not present, return (-1, -1).
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="First and last occurrence"><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">First and Last Occurrence</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Find one answer, then push left for first and right for last</text><g font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="52" y="126" width="92" height="64" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="98" y="168" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="156" y="116" width="92" height="84" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.34)"/><text x="202" y="168" text-anchor="middle">2</text><rect x="260" y="116" width="92" height="84" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.34)"/><text x="306" y="168" text-anchor="middle">2</text><rect x="364" y="116" width="92" height="84" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.34)"/><text x="410" y="168" text-anchor="middle">2</text><rect x="468" y="126" width="92" height="64" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="514" y="168" text-anchor="middle">3</text><rect x="572" y="126" width="92" height="64" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="618" y="168" text-anchor="middle">4</text></g><text x="170" y="234" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">first = 1</text><text x="360" y="234" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">last = 3</text><path d="M 202 94 L 202 70" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4"/><path d="M 410 94 L 410 70" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4"/><text x="720" y="150" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Run binary search twice</text><text x="720" y="182" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">one for left boundary</text><text x="720" y="210" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">one for right boundary</text></svg>
hints: Binary search once is not enough.
To find first occurrence, continue searching left after finding target.
To find last occurrence, continue searching right after finding target.
Write a helper function for left/right boundary.

questions.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
questions.prompt:
Given a sorted array rotated at some pivot, search for a target in O(log n) time.

Return its index if found, otherwise return -1.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1020 340" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Search in rotated sorted array"><rect width="1020" height="340" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Search in Rotated Sorted Array</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">At least one side of mid is always sorted</text><g font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="42" y="130" width="90" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="87" y="170" text-anchor="middle">4</text><rect x="144" y="130" width="90" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="189" y="170" text-anchor="middle">5</text><rect x="246" y="130" width="90" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="291" y="170" text-anchor="middle">6</text><rect x="348" y="120" width="90" height="82" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.34)"/><text x="393" y="170" text-anchor="middle">7</text><rect x="450" y="130" width="90" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.22)"/><text x="495" y="170" text-anchor="middle">0</text><rect x="552" y="130" width="90" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="597" y="170" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="654" y="130" width="90" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="699" y="170" text-anchor="middle">2</text></g><text x="348" y="232" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">mid = 7</text><text x="808" y="134" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Observation</text><text x="808" y="170" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Left half [4,5,6,7]</text><text x="808" y="198" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">is sorted</text><text x="808" y="234" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Target 0 is not there,</text><text x="808" y="262" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">so search right half</text></svg>
hints: Find mid as usual.
Check whether left half is sorted or right half is sorted.
Decide whether the target lies inside that sorted half.
Discard the other half.
questions.starter_code:
def search_rotated(arr, target):
    # TODO
    return -1

print(search_rotated([4,5,6,7,0,1,2], 0))
print(search_rotated([4,5,6,7,0,1,2], 3))

questions.title: Find Peak Element
questions.prompt:
A peak element is greater than its neighbors.

Given an array, return the index of any peak element.
You may assume arr[-1] and arr[n] are negative infinity.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 330" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Find peak element"><rect width="980" height="330" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Find Peak Element</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Move toward the rising slope. A peak must exist there.</text><polyline points="70,230 190,170 310,90 430,230" fill="none" stroke="#ff9f1c" stroke-width="8" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><circle cx="310" cy="90" r="16" fill="#ff7b00"/><text x="310" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">peak</text><text x="70" y="272" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">1</text><text x="190" y="272" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">2</text><text x="310" y="272" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">3</text><text x="430" y="272" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">1</text><rect x="610" y="108" width="300" height="132" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="634" y="140" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Binary-search idea</text><text x="634" y="176" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">if arr[mid] < arr[mid+1]</text><text x="634" y="204" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">go right</text><text x="634" y="232" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">else go left or stay</text></svg>
hints: Compare arr[mid] with arr[mid+1].
If arr[mid] < arr[mid+1], then a peak exists on the right.
Otherwise a peak exists on the left side including mid.
Use binary search on this condition.
questions.starter_code:
def find_peak(arr):
    # TODO
    return -1

print(find_peak([1, 2, 3, 1]))
print(find_peak([1, 2, 1, 3, 5, 6, 4]))

questions.title: Search on Answer — Minimum Eating Speed
questions.prompt:
Koko loves bananas. There are several piles, and Koko can decide an eating speed k bananas per hour.

Given the piles and the number of hours h, find the minimum integer k such that Koko can finish all bananas within h hours.

This is binary search on the answer space, not on the input array.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1040 360" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Search on answer minimum eating speed"><rect width="1040" height="360" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Search on Answer — Minimum Eating Speed</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Binary search the answer space: low = 1, high = max(piles)</text><g font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="54" y="156" width="88" height="92" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.22)"/><text x="98" y="210" text-anchor="middle">3</text><rect x="164" y="126" width="88" height="122" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.22)"/><text x="208" y="196" text-anchor="middle">6</text><rect x="274" y="116" width="88" height="132" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.22)"/><text x="318" y="190" text-anchor="middle">7</text><rect x="384" y="76" width="88" height="172" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.34)"/><text x="428" y="170" text-anchor="middle">11</text></g><text x="52" y="292" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">piles = [3, 6, 7, 11]</text><rect x="600" y="102" width="370" height="166" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="624" y="136" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Try speed = 4</text><text x="624" y="172" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">hours = ceil(3/4) + ceil(6/4) +</text><text x="624" y="200" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">ceil(7/4) + ceil(11/4)</text><text x="624" y="236" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">1 + 2 + 2 + 3 = 8</text><text x="624" y="264" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">valid → try smaller answer</text><text x="30" y="336" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Key idea: if speed works, search left; otherwise search right.</text></svg>
hints: Answer lies between 1 and max(piles).
For a candidate speed k, compute total hours needed.
If total hours <= h, k works, so try a smaller speed.
Otherwise increase k.
questions.starter_code:

def min_eating_speed(piles, h):
    # TODO
    return 0

print(min_eating_speed([3, 6, 7, 11], 8))
print(min_eating_speed([30, 11, 23, 4, 20], 5))
